title: The Best SaaS Marketing Strategy for 2026

segments.text: In this video, I'm going to break down
the exact [music] marketing strategy
that the 8 figureure B2B SAS companies I
consult with are using right now to
scale. The results we're seeing with
this strategy are truly incredible.
We've helped companies scale from 5
million to 50 million in ARR and even
got one company to 160 million in ARR.
And what's even crazier that this
strategy works for all mid-market and
enterprise B2B SAS companies, anyone
with an ACV over $1,000. By the end of
this video, you'll understand what this
strategy is and how you can start
implementing it to grow your B2B SAS
company starting right now. If you don't
know who I am, my name is Ryan Alice. I
built my own SAS company I contact to
70,000 paying customers, 50 million in
ARR, and I sold it for $169
million. I now consult with CEOs and
founders of other SAS firms like
instantly clear stream to Tango Seamless
many others and have helped them scale
from as small as 3 to 4 million in ARR
up to 100 million plus in ARR. So here's
the old way SAS marketing was done. They
either go allin on paid ads without any
strategy. They're just throwing money at
Meta or Google without any coordination.
or they try to do outbound email, but
they're sending generic messages to
random lists they bought. That's not the
way to do it. The problem with this
approach, the old approach, is that
you're just wasting money. You're not
going to get results. You're getting bad
leads. Your CAC is too high, and you're
not doing attribution properly, and
you're certainly not building any
thoughtful brand awareness within your
target market, within your ICP. main
difference from a 3 millionaire company
to a 30 millionaire company, it's not
the product, it's actually the marketing
system and it's whether you can figure
out the holy grail of digital ads. Let's
reveal the new way and let me share with
you what's actually working right now
for our clients. At SAS drives, we
manage $900,000 a month of B2B ad spend.
We have a community of 400 SAS CEOs and
founders that are part of our mastermind
calls that we put on every week. And
over the past two years, we've developed
with our 400 members our B2B SAS growth
system. Let me walk you through it step
by step by step. This strategy works
well for any B2B firm where the ACV, the
annual contract value is between $1,000
a year and $500,000 a year. So high SMB
to mid-market all the way up to
enterprise B2B SAS companies. Pay close
attention. There are seven steps to
massive revenue scaling in B2B SAS. Step
one is building your accountbased
marketing lead list, your ABM lead list.
It's the foundation of everything. You
need to identify everyone in your target
market. I'm talking about the exact job
titles, industries, company sizes, and
the geographies that you sell to. Then
you go out and build a comprehensive
list of everyone who fits the criteria.
You do it on tools like Apollo, Listkit,
Instantly, Seamless, LinkedIn Sales
Navigator. Then you do everything else.
You do your matched audience ads. You do
your outbound email. You do your
LinkedIn outreach. It's your single
source of targeting truth for your
marketing. Now, what do you do? You just
search for the job titles, the
geographies, the company sizes, the
industries that you target, the keywords
if you want, and then you export the
full results to a CSV file. You import
the CSV file into Meta. You turn on ads
to that matched audience. And once you
have the matched audience ads turned on,
then you start taking the same exact CSV
file, import it into Instantly, and
start sending out outbound emails. So
now you have omniresence. You have
people seeing your ads everywhere
through matched ads and retargeting ads.
And then you have everyone getting your
messaging through outbound email and
outbound LinkedIn. When you combine
these matched audience ads with other ad
types like retargeting, lookike, and
paid search, you can really scale up
customer acquisition. Now, let's go into
step two. It's your weekly content
machine. Your weekly content marketing
machine. It's non-negotiable. You need
to produce at least one quality piece of
content per week for your blog, for your
newsletter, for your YouTube. It could
be a case study, it could be a webinar,
it could be a PDF report, it could be a
video like this. The key criteria across
all of these is that it must actually be
valuable to your audience. It needs to
educate your prospects. It needs to show
that you understand the problems and
challenges that they face. This video
we're doing right now is not going to be
useful to a school teacher in Iowa, but
it's going to be extremely useful to a
B2B SAS CEO and founder. So, you take
your inner knowledge, the inside
knowledge of the challenges your market
faces, and then you distribute that
content on multiple channels to your
email list, to your blog. You send it
out in your weekly newsletter every
Monday and then you create ads around it
by taking a screenshot of the piece of
content and putting up in a retargeting
ad. You can easily turn a video
transcript from a video like this into a
blog post by just plugging in the
transcript into chat GBT and saying,
"Make me a 2,000word blog post and
adding a couple photos from a slide
deck." And you can easily turn blog
posts into videos using tools like
Synthesia. The goal is to get 1 million
content impressions per month within
your ICP across ads, web visits, warm
email, cold email, LinkedIn, and
YouTube. That's how to set up a B2B
marketing machine. So, now we've covered
how to set up your ABM lead list, and
we've covered how to set up your content
machine. Now, let's go on to step three,
which is how to launch outbound
marketing. This is where AI
personalization comes in. You take your
ABM lead list you created in step one
and you use a tool like instantly to
write personalized subject lines and
opening lines and even PS lines for
every person on your list. We send these
out with three to five step sequences
about 2 to 3 days apart. And we
alternate between a focus on clicks and
a focus on replies. Now why do we do
this when most people in cold email say
only do replies? We focus on clicks and
replies because when you can get someone
to click, then you can get them in the
retargeting ad audience and then make
your brand omniresence and follow up
automatically. And you can also export
that list, that CSV file of all the
clicks, send it to your sales
development rep, and they can follow up
via phone or email or LinkedIn. All
right, so why go for clicks sometimes is
so that your targeting ads and your SDRs
can follow up and make your brand
omniresent within your target market.
And then you layer in LinkedIn outreach,
automated LinkedIn outreach on top. When
someone clicks on the email, you set up
a web hook or import the CSV file to Hey
Reach and you use Hey Reach to set up a
LinkedIn connection request. And when
that's accepted, Hey Reach will send a
personalized voice note and AI
customized message sequence to the
prospects. And now you're hitting them
on multiple channels. You're hitting
them on email, you're hitting them on
ads, you're hitting them on LinkedIn.
You're starting to build awareness of
what you solve, the problem that you
solve in the marketplace. This process
creates multiple touch points and that
is the key to making your brand truly
omnipresent and it's crucial in this day
and age when there's so much noise. Step
four is to set up conversion tracking.
This is because you need to know your
actual CAC and your target CAC. To
determine your target CAC, just figure
out what your ACV is, your annual
contract value, and divide by two. How
much do you get per year? Divide by two.
That'd be a sixmonth payback. And that's
a pretty good average. You could also
take one six of lifetime revenue or
lifetime value. That's a good way to
estimate your target CAC. Now, once you
know your target CAC, you figure out
what your target cost per lead should
be, which is just your target CAC
multiplied times your conversion rate.
And then you figure out what it is that
the actual cost per lead and actual cost
per qualified lead and actual CAC is
going to be from the ad networks. We'll
walk you through that just now. What are
the ad networks that you're going to
want to use? Meta, LinkedIn, Google, and
Bing. You can use a tool like Cometly,
which we highly recommend, to track
which channels and which ad types are
driving qualified leads and customers.
And that's way better because if you
just rely on the ad network's internal
ad tracking tools, they're going to take
credit for the same customer. You're
going to be doing retargeting on Meta,
LinkedIn, and Google. And all three of
those networks are going to say, "Hey, I
showed it out of that person, then they
signed up. Give me credit." So you need
a tool like Cometly to disagregate and
ddup the conversions so that one
customer shows up as one customer not
three. The other thing that a tool like
Cometly does is it uses Cappy conversion
API to send back the conversion data
back into Google and Bing and LinkedIn
and Meta so that you can very easily
train the algorithm on who is a good
customer for you so they can send more
people like that. Let's move on to step
five, which is launching your ads. And
this is where most people mess up. They
try to do all the ad types at once. And
I'm telling you right now, don't do it
like that. Do it sequentially, step by
step by step, and do it in the following
order. First, you need to turn on
retargeting ads. That makes sure your
brand is omniresent across New York
Times, ESPN, across Facebook, across
Instagram. So, turn on retargeting ads
on Google Display, turn on retargeting
ads on Meta, and turn on retargeting ads
on LinkedIn. anyone who visits your
website in the last 180 days, you show
them ads repeatedly. Once you have that
going, then you do matched audience ads.
You take your ABM lead list that you
built earlier and you upload it to Meta,
LinkedIn, and Google. And then you'll
get a match rate. The match rate will be
between 30 and 60%, usually. You can
increase the match rate by enriching
your list with personal email addresses
or even phone numbers. You could do that
on clay.com. And then you show ads to
that exact audience that you just
uploaded. And now for the third layer,
once you're doing retargeting and
matched audience ads, you want to do
lookalike ads on Meta and you create
your list of your best customers, your
best prospects, your best demos. You
need at least a couple thousand records
and you upload that as a lookalike
audience. And then what Meta will do is
it'll show ads to people that
psychographically, formographically,
demographically look like those people.
Fourth, you do paid search on Bing and
Google for your both your brand terms,
your competitor brand terms as well as
your market terms. Finally, for your
fifth layer, you do thought leader ads
on LinkedIn where you boost your organic
post to your matched audiences and you
layer on LinkedIn sponsored message ads
that go directly to the inbox of your
recipients on LinkedIn and that is to
your matched audience prospects. This
stepbystep process, this formula is how
you make your brand omniresent across
multiple channels in B2B marketing. If
you want to learn more about how we're
doing this, check us out at
sasrise.com/growth
program. We have a 16week program where
we take you and your marketing team
through this step by step by step by
step. That one costs 5,000 a month for 4
months. Now that you have your ads
going, we got to tell you how to scale
it up. Cuz most people, they'll turn on
ads, they'll give it a try, they'll fail
after one or two months, and then
they'll say, "You know what? I'm just
giving up. The key here is that you have
to do conversion rate optimization and
budget optimization. So step six to this
whole process is scaling what works. You
track your cost per lead and you track
your cost per qualified lead and you
track your CPA, otherwise known as your
cost per acquisition, your customer
acquisition cost, same thing. And you do
that for every ad network in every ad
type. Now, what is a cost per lead? It's
just ad spend divided by leads. What's
cost per qualified lead? Ad spend
divided by qualified leads. That could
be sales accepted leads, SQLs,
opportunities, whatever you consider to
be a qualified lead. And once you have
your cost per qualified lead per
channel, then you just put more budget
into the channels that are generating
the lowest cost, cost per qualified
lead. And you just scale budget on the
channels that are working best while
keeping in mind the delineation between
demand generation and demand capture
where demand generation often happens on
Meta and LinkedIn and demand capture
often happens on Google and Bing. And so
if your cost per acquisition is a little
bit higher on Meta or LinkedIn than it
is on say Google or Bing, that's okay
because a lot of times it's the display
ads that generate the awareness and then
the paid search ads that capture the
awareness once it's actually generated.
A good tool like Cometly can sort that
out for you. Now what you want to do is
you want to make sure you focus on
conversion rate optimization for your
landing page and ads. Now that all comes
down to what your offer is. So don't
make your call to action a demo. Maybe
on a later retargeting ad, that's fine.
But on your initial awareness ads, you
want to make your call to action
something that's a little bit easier to
get them to show up for a live call. So
don't make your CTA demo. That's too far
down the funnel. No one wants to get on
a sales call at the very beginning of a
sales process. That's for later.
Instead, use short recorded 5 to 10
minute demos. Interactive demos made
with Story Lane are also great. Or if
you have a PLG motion, use a trial and
free account. That's a great way. even
tools like play or instantly. You can
just get a free account and play around
and then upgrade if you like it. You can
use high quality content pieces as well
as lead magnets. So create some
highquality PDF case studies, reports,
take responses from your customers or
your surveys and then put that out to
rest the prospects in your similar
industry. Put those content pieces out
as what you're advertising or free
accounts, free trials or interactive
demos. That's way better than
advertising a demo. By not going for the
kill right away, you can build up trust,
educate your prospects 2 3 months down
the line, then they're going to reach
out to you for that sales call. And
lastly, you methodically work to
optimize every step in the funnel from
ad click-through rate, visitor lead
conversion, lead conversion to qualified
lead conversion, qualified lead to
opportunity conversion, and lastly,
opportunity to closed one conversion.
And you do that through a mix of
marketing automation via email and SMS
and ringless voicemails and through your
SDR team doing manual reachouts. An
unoptimized funnel might have a CAC of
$50,000 or $100,000 whereas an optimized
funnel built with this system could have
a CAC between a,000 and $10,000. A 10x
difference. And that's the difference
between a company that can scale to 50
million AR and a company that doesn't.
If you want to check out more of the
training I have here on YouTube, check
out the next video where I break down
how Clay.com is making over a hundred
million a year in revenue and how they
got to a $3 billion valuation. Hope you
have a wonderful rest of your day. Check
us out at sassrise.com.
